purpose: Pinned pipeline for WS5 emitted-proof-size measurements on the R(3,k) upper-half family. The measured quantity is S(F; encoding, solver, config, seed) — the size of the proof THIS pipeline emits. It is an achievable upper bound on proof length under this pipeline, NOT a minimal certificate; no claim about shortest proofs is made (charter section 4, WS5).

encoding.name: r3k-edge-cnf
encoding.description: Variables = edges of K_n in lexicographic order (true = red). Clauses: one all-negative clause per 3-subset (no red triangle), one all-positive clause per k-subset (no blue K_k); red block then blue block, each in lexicographic subset order. Deterministic; unit-tested against certificates/ramsey-3-3/problem.cnf (logically identical clause set; not byte-identical).

solver.name: cadical
solver.config: default options; plain-text DRAT forced with --no-binary; seed set with --seed
solver.seed_mechanism: cadical's built-in --seed=<n> option (present in this version, so no clause-shuffling workaround is used). Observed: at R(3,3) n=6 the run is seed-invariant (byte-identical proofs across seeds 1-3); at R(3,4) n=9 seeds produce distinct proofs.

checker.name: drat-trim
checker.build: cc -O2 -o drat-trim drat-trim.c (from a clean checkout of the pinned commit)
checker.verdict_rule: PASS iff stdout contains the substring 's VERIFIED'. The exit code is never trusted (drat-trim can exit 0 on both outcomes), and the substring is not anchored at line start (progress output uses carriage returns).
checker.negative_control: a deliberately truncated proof must yield 's NOT VERIFIED'

byte_size_definition: emitted_drat_bytes = the size in bytes (st_size) of the plain-text DRAT file exactly as written by the solver invocation above. No trimming (drat-trim's optimized core is NOT the measured object), no compression, no post-processing.
timing_definition: solve_seconds_wall and check_seconds_wall are wall-clock seconds for the solver/checker process on one arm64 macOS machine (Darwin 24.5.0); indicative only, not a pinned quantity.

caps_for_attempts.note: an aborted run is recorded as DNF — a result, not a gap
